login.title: Login

login.ssoEnabledNotice: Single Sign-On (SSO) is enabled. LiteLLM no longer automatically redirects to the SSO login flow upon loading this page. To re-enable auto-redirect-to-SSO, set AUTO_REDIRECT_UI_LOGIN_TO_SSO=true in your environment configuration.

createdKeyDisplay.saveWarning: Please save this secret key somewhere safe and accessible. For security reasons, you will not be able to view it again through your LiteLLM account. If you lose this secret key, you will need to generate a new one.

modelsColumns.reusableDescription: Credentials saved in LiteLLM that can be added to models repeatedly.

modelsColumns.configNoPause: Config models cannot be paused from the dashboard. Pause is DB-backed.

modelsColumns.configNoDelete: Config model cannot be deleted on the dashboard. Please delete it from the config file.

keyLifecycleSettings.expireKeyTooltip: Set when this key should expire. Format: 30s (seconds), 30m (minutes), 30h (hours), 30d (days). Leave empty to keep the current expiry unchanged.
keyLifecycleSettings.expireKeyPlaceholderCreate: e.g., 30d or leave empty to never expire
keyLifecycleSettings.enableAutoRotationTooltip: Key will automatically regenerate at the specified interval for enhanced security.
keyLifecycleSettings.rotationIntervalTooltip: How often the key should be automatically rotated. Choose the interval that best fits your security requirements.
keyLifecycleSettings.customIntervalFormats: Supported formats: seconds (s), minutes (m), hours (h), days (d)
keyLifecycleSettings.rotationNotice: When rotation occurs, you'll receive a notification with the new key. The old key will be deactivated after a brief grace period.

passThroughGuardrailsSection.title: Guardrails
passThroughGuardrailsSection.subtitle: Configure guardrails to enforce policies on requests and responses. Guardrails are opt-in for passthrough endpoints.
passThroughGuardrailsSection.fieldLevelDesc: Optionally specify which fields to check. If left empty, the entire request/response is sent to the guardrail.
passThroughGuardrailsSection.selectGuardrailsTooltip: Choose which guardrails should run on this endpoint. Org/team/key level guardrails will also be included.
passThroughGuardrailsSection.leaveEmptyTip: Tip: Leave empty to check entire payload
passThroughGuardrailsSection.requestFieldsTooltipTitle: Specify which request fields to check
passThroughGuardrailsSection.responseFieldsTooltipTitle: Specify which response fields to check
passThroughGuardrailsSection.requestFieldsPlaceholder: Type field name or use + buttons above (e.g., query, documents[*].text)
passThroughGuardrailsSection.responseFieldsPlaceholder: Type field name or use + buttons above (e.g., results[*].text)

passThroughSecuritySection.title: Security
passThroughSecuritySection.subtitle: When enabled, requests to this endpoint will require a valid LiteLLM Virtual Key
passThroughSecuritySection.enterpriseNotice: Setting authentication for pass-through endpoints is a LiteLLM Enterprise feature. Get a trial key <pricingLink>here</pricingLink>.

premiumLoggingSettings.enterpriseNotice: Setting Key/Team logging settings is a LiteLLM Enterprise feature. Global Logging Settings are available for all free users. Get a trial key <pricingLink>here</pricingLink>.

rateLimitTypeFormItem.label: {{limitTypeUpper}} Rate Limit Type
rateLimitTypeFormItem.tooltip: Select 'guaranteed_throughput' to prevent overallocating {{limitTypeUpper}} limit when the key belongs to a Team with specific {{limitTypeUpper}} limits.
rateLimitTypeFormItem.bestEffortDesc: Best effort throughput - no error if we're overallocating {{limitTypeLower}} (Team/Key Limits checked at runtime).
rateLimitTypeFormItem.guaranteedThroughputDesc: Guaranteed throughput - raise an error if we're overallocating {{limitTypeLower}} (also checks model-specific limits)
rateLimitTypeFormItem.dynamicDesc: If the key has a set {{limitTypeUpper}} (e.g. 2 {{limitTypeUpper}}) and there are no 429 errors, it can dynamically exceed the limit when the model being called is not erroring.

form.organizationTooltipPrefix: Organizations can have multiple teams. Learn more about

form.teamIdHelp: ID of the team you want to create. If not provided, it will be generated automatically.
form.teamMemberBudgetTooltip: This is the individual budget for a user in the team.
form.teamMemberKeyDurationTooltip: Set a limit to the duration of a team member's key. Format: 30s (seconds), 30m (minutes), 30h (hours), 30d (days), 1mo (month)
form.teamMemberRpmLimitTooltip: The RPM (Requests Per Minute) limit for individual team members

form.secretManagerSettingsPremiumHelp: Premium feature - Upgrade to manage secret manager settings.

form.disableGlobalGuardrailsHelp: Bypass global guardrails for this team
form.disableGlobalGuardrailsPremium: Premium feature - Upgrade to disable global guardrails by team
form.policiesTooltip: Apply policies to this team to control guardrails and other settings

form.accessGroupsTooltip: Assign access groups to this team. Access groups control which models, MCP servers, and agents this team can use

form.modelAliasesDescription: Create custom aliases for models that can be used by team members in API calls. This allows you to create shortcuts for specific models.

scim.title: SCIM Configuration
scim.description: System for Cross-domain Identity Management (SCIM) allows you to automatically provision and manage users and groups in LiteLLM.

scim.tokenWarning: Make sure to copy this token now. You will not be able to see it again.

teamSsoSettings.teamMemberPermissionsDesc: Default permissions granted to members of newly created teams. /key/info and /key/health are always included.

addPassThrough.alertDescription: Route requests from your LiteLLM proxy to any external API. Perfect for custom models, image generation APIs, or any service you want to proxy through LiteLLM.
addPassThrough.routeConfigSubtitle: Configure how requests to your domain will be forwarded to the target API
addPassThrough.httpMethodsTooltip: Select specific HTTP methods. Leave empty to support all methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH). Useful when the same path needs different targets for different methods.
addPassThrough.selectedMethodsDesc: Only {{methods}} requests will be routed to this endpoint
addPassThrough.includeSubpathsDesc: Forward all subpaths to the target API (recommended for REST APIs)
addPassThrough.headersSectionSubtitle: Add headers that will be sent with every request to the target API
addPassThrough.authHeadersTooltip: Authentication and other headers to forward with requests
addPassThrough.headersExtraTitle: Add authentication tokens and other required headers
addPassThrough.queryParamsSectionSubtitle: Add query parameters that will be automatically sent with every request to the target API
addPassThrough.queryParamsTooltip: Query parameters that will be added to all requests. Clients can override these by providing their own values.
addPassThrough.queryParamsExtraTitle: Parameters are sent with all GET, POST, PUT, PATCH requests
addPassThrough.queryParamsExtraExamples: Client parameters override defaults. Examples: version=v1, format=json, key=default
addPassThrough.billingSectionSubtitle: Optional cost tracking for this endpoint
addPassThrough.costPerRequestTooltip: Optional: Track costs for requests to this endpoint
addPassThrough.costPerRequestExtra: The cost charged for each request through this endpoint

agents.healthCheckTooltip: When enabled, only agents with reachable URLs are shown
agents.noAgentsFound: No agents found. Click "+ Add New Agent" to create one.

bulkCreateUsers.step1Desc: Add multiple users at once by following these steps:
bulkCreateUsers.step1Item2: Add your users' information to the spreadsheet

bulkCreateUsers.parseErrorHint1: Check the table below for specific errors in each row
bulkCreateUsers.parseErrorHint2: Common issues include invalid email formats, missing required fields, or incorrect role values
bulkCreateUsers.parseErrorHint3: Fix these issues in your CSV file and upload again
bulkCreateUsers.nextStepDesc: Download the credentials file containing Virtual Keys and invitation links. Users will need these Virtual Keys to make LLM requests through LiteLLM.
bulkCreateUsers.invalidFileType: Invalid file type: {{name}}. Please upload a CSV file (.csv extension).
bulkCreateUsers.invalidFileTypeShort: Invalid file type. Please upload a CSV file.
bulkCreateUsers.fileTooLarge: File is too large ({{size}} MB). Please upload a CSV file smaller than 5MB.
bulkCreateUsers.csvEmpty: The CSV file appears to be empty. Please upload a file with data.
bulkCreateUsers.csvHeaderOnly: The CSV file only contains headers but no user data. Please add user data to your CSV.
bulkCreateUsers.csvNoHeaders: The CSV file doesn't contain any column headers. Please make sure your CSV has headers.
bulkCreateUsers.csvMissingColumns: Your CSV is missing these required columns: {{columns}}. Please add these columns to your CSV file.
bulkCreateUsers.rowTooFewColumns: Row {{row}} has fewer columns than the header row. Please ensure all data is properly formatted.
bulkCreateUsers.emailInvalid: Invalid email format (must contain @ and domain)
bulkCreateUsers.roleInvalid: Invalid role "{{role}}". Must be one of: {{validRoles}}
bulkCreateUsers.budgetNotNumber: Max budget "{{budget}}" must be a number
bulkCreateUsers.budgetMustBePositive: Max budget must be greater than 0
bulkCreateUsers.budgetDurationInvalid: Invalid budget duration format "{{duration}}". Use format like "30d", "1mo", "2w", "6h"
bulkCreateUsers.noDataRows: No valid data rows found in the CSV file. Please check your file format.
bulkCreateUsers.noValidUsers: No valid users found in the CSV. Please check the errors below and fix your CSV file.

guardrails.deleteGuardrailMessage: Are you sure you want to delete guardrail: {{name}}? This action cannot be undone.

teamGuardrailsTab.forwardKeyDesc: When enabled, the caller's LiteLLM API key is forwarded as an Authorization header to your guardrail endpoint. This allows your guardrail to authenticate model calls using the original caller's credentials.
teamGuardrailsTab.staticHeadersDesc: Sent with every request to the guardrail.
teamGuardrailsTab.forwardClientHeadersDesc: Allowed header names to forward from the client request to the guardrail (e.g. x-request-id).
teamGuardrailsTab.guardrailInfoNote: This guardrail runs on a separate instance. It receives the user request and forwards the result to the next step in the pipeline. See <docsLink>LiteLLM Generic Guardrail API docs</docsLink> for configuration details.
teamGuardrailsTab.approveGuardrailConfirm: Are you sure you want to approve "{{name}}"? This will make it active and available for use.
teamGuardrailsTab.rejectGuardrailConfirm: Are you sure you want to reject "{{name}}"? This will mark it as rejected and notify the team.
teamGuardrailsTab.failedToUpdateForwardApiKey: Failed to update forward API key
teamGuardrailsTab.failedToUpdateForwardClientHeaders: Failed to update forward client headers
teamGuardrailsTab.submitCallout: Your guardrail will be sent for admin review before it becomes active.
teamGuardrailsTab.formExtraParamsTooltip: JSON object merged into litellm_params. e.g. forward_api_key, headers, model, unreachable_fallback

addGuardrailForm.guardrailNamePlaceholder: Enter a name for this guardrail
addGuardrailForm.modeTooltip: How the guardrail should be applied
addGuardrailForm.alwaysOnTooltip: If enabled, this guardrail will be applied to all requests by default.
addGuardrailForm.skipSystemMsgTooltip: Unified guardrails only: omit role: system from guardrail evaluation input (OpenAI chat + Anthropic messages). The model still receives full messages. Use global default follows litellm_settings.skip_system_message_in_guardrail.
addGuardrailForm.skipToolMsgTooltip: Unified guardrails only: omit role: tool from guardrail evaluation input (OpenAI chat + Anthropic messages). The model still receives full messages. Use global default follows litellm_settings.skip_tool_message_in_guardrail.

modeDesc.pre_call: Before LLM Call - Runs before the LLM call and checks the input (Recommended)
modeDesc.during_call: During LLM Call - Runs in parallel with the LLM call, with response held until check completes
modeDesc.post_call: After LLM Call - Runs after the LLM call and checks only the output
modeDesc.logging_only: Logging Only - Only runs on logging callbacks without affecting the LLM call
modeDesc.pre_mcp_call: Before MCP Tool Call - Runs before MCP tool execution and validates tool calls
modeDesc.during_mcp_call: During MCP Tool Call - Runs in parallel with MCP tool execution for monitoring

addGuardrailForm.endpointSettingsDesc: Configure settings for a specific call type. Most guardrails don't need this — skip it unless you're using a specific endpoint like /v1/realtime.
addGuardrailForm.endSessionDesc: Automatically close the session after this many guardrail violations. Leave empty to never auto-close.
addGuardrailForm.violationWarnDesc: Bot speaks the message, session continues
addGuardrailForm.violationEndSessionDesc: Bot speaks the message, connection closes immediately
addGuardrailForm.violationMessageDesc: What the bot says aloud when this guardrail fires. Falls back to the default violation message if empty.
addGuardrailForm.violationMessagePlaceholder: e.g. I'm not able to continue this conversation. Please contact us at 1-800-774-2678.
addGuardrailForm.selectAtLeastOnePii: Please select at least one PII entity to continue
addGuardrailForm.configureAtLeastOneFilter: Please configure at least one content filter setting (category, pattern, keyword, or competitor intent)
addGuardrailForm.addAtLeastOneCriterion: Add at least one evaluation criterion
addGuardrailForm.addAtLeastOneRule: Add at least one tool permission rule

guardrailOptionalParams.title: Optional Parameters
guardrailOptionalParams.defaultDescription: Configure additional settings for this guardrail provider
guardrailOptionalParams.selectCategoryHint: Select a category to add threshold configuration

guardrailProviderFields.noFields: No configuration fields available for this provider.

guardrailTable.configDeleteTooltip: Config guardrail cannot be deleted on the dashboard. Please delete it from the config file.

lLMJudgeFields.description: After each LLM response, the Judge Model scores it 0–100 against your criteria. If the weighted average falls below the threshold, the response is blocked (or logged).
lLMJudgeFields.judgeModelTooltip: The LLM that reads each response and grades it. Pick a capable model — it never sees end-user data beyond what the LLM returned.
lLMJudgeFields.minScoreTooltip: 0–100. If the weighted average of criterion scores falls below this, the guardrail triggers. 80 is a good default.
lLMJudgeFields.onFailureTooltip: Block: return HTTP 422 when the score is too low. Log: record the result but let the response through.
lLMJudgeFields.criteriaTooltip: Each criterion is something the judge checks. Weights must add up to 100%.
lLMJudgeFields.weightTooltip: How much this criterion counts toward the final score. All weights must add up to 100%.
lLMJudgeFields.criterionDescPlaceholder: What should the judge check for this criterion?

piiComponents.quickActionsTooltip: Apply action to all PII types at once
piiComponents.noMatch: No PII types match your filter criteria

toolPermissionRulesEditor.title: LiteLLM Tool Permission Guardrail
toolPermissionRulesEditor.subtitle: Provide regex patterns (e.g., ^mcp__github_.*$) for tool names or types and optionally constrain payload fields.
toolPermissionRulesEditor.onDisallowedTooltip: Block returns an error when a forbidden tool is invoked. Rewrite strips the tool call but lets the rest of the response continue.

competitorIntentConfiguration.title: Competitor Intent Filter
competitorIntentConfiguration.disabledDescription: Block or reframe competitor comparison questions. When enabled, airline type auto-loads competitors from IATA; generic type requires manual competitor list.
competitorIntentConfiguration.enabledDescription: Block or reframe competitor comparison questions. Airline type uses major airlines (excluding your brand); generic requires manual competitor list.
competitorIntentConfiguration.brandSelfHelpAirline: Select your airline from the list (excluded from competitors) or type to add a custom term
competitorIntentConfiguration.brandSelfHelpGeneric: Names/codes users use for your brand
competitorIntentConfiguration.brandSelfPlaceholderAirline: Search or select airline, or type to add custom
competitorIntentConfiguration.brandSelfPlaceholderGeneric: Type and press Enter to add
competitorIntentConfiguration.locationsHelp: Countries, cities, airports for disambiguation (e.g. qatar, doha)
competitorIntentConfiguration.competitorsHelp: Competitor names to detect (required for generic type)
competitorIntentConfiguration.tagsPlaceholder: Type and press Enter to add
competitorIntentConfiguration.confidenceThresholdsHelp: Classify competitor intent by confidence (0–1). Higher confidence → stronger intent.
competitorIntentConfiguration.thresholdHighDesc: Treat as full competitor comparison → uses "Competitor comparison" policy
competitorIntentConfiguration.thresholdMediumDesc: Treat as possible comparison → uses "Possible competitor comparison" policy
competitorIntentConfiguration.thresholdLowDesc: Log only; allow request. Below Low → allow with no action
competitorIntentConfiguration.thresholdsNote: Raise thresholds to be more permissive; lower them to be stricter.

editGuardrailForm.skipSystemMsgTooltip: Unified guardrails only: whether role: system content is omitted from guardrail input (LLM still receives full messages). Use global default follows litellm_settings.skip_system_message_in_guardrail.
editGuardrailForm.skipToolMsgTooltip: Unified guardrails only: whether role: tool content is omitted from guardrail input (LLM still receives full messages). Use global default follows litellm_settings.skip_tool_message_in_guardrail.

guardrailGarden.litellmSectionDesc: Built-in guardrails powered by LiteLLM. Zero latency, no external dependencies, no additional cost.
guardrailGarden.partnerSectionDesc: Third-party guardrail integrations from leading AI security providers.

guardrailInfo.configGuardrailTooltip: Guardrail is defined in the config file and cannot be edited.
guardrailInfo.skipSystemMsgTooltip: Unified guardrails: omit role: system from guardrail input (LLM still gets full messages). Use global default follows litellm_settings.skip_system_message_in_guardrail.
guardrailInfo.skipToolMsgTooltip: Unified guardrails: omit role: tool from guardrail input (LLM still gets full messages). Use global default follows litellm_settings.skip_tool_message_in_guardrail.

byokCredentialModal.connectDesc: LiteLLM needs access to {{name}} to complete your request.
byokCredentialModal.howItWorksDesc: LiteLLM acts as a secure bridge. Your requests are routed through our MCP client directly to {{name}}'s API.
byokCredentialModal.provideApiKeyDesc: Enter your {{name}} API key to authorize this connection.
byokCredentialModal.whereIsMyApiKey: Where do I find my API key?
byokCredentialModal.securityNote: Your key is stored securely and transmitted over HTTPS. It is never shared with third parties.

envVarsSection.tooltipDefine: Define variables you can interpolate in Static Headers or Authentication using
envVarsSection.tooltipInstance: admin-defined value used for every user.
envVarsSection.tooltipPerUser: each user supplies their own value (e.g. personal credentials) via the MCP Gateway dashboard.
envVarsSection.referenceHint: Reference these in Static Headers or Authentication as
envVarsSection.varNamePattern: Use letters, digits, underscores; cannot start with a digit.
envVarsSection.perUserHintTooltip: Per-user variables have no shared value. This text is only a hint shown to each user when they fill in their own value.

mcpLogoSelector.logoTooltip: Select a well-known logo or paste a URL to any image. The logo is shown on the admin and chat pages.
mcpLogoSelector.customUrlPlaceholder: Or paste a custom logo URL...

mcpNetworkSettings.privateIpRangesDesc: Define which IP ranges are part of your private network. Callers from these IPs can see all MCP servers. Callers from any other IP can only see servers marked "Available on Public Internet".
mcpNetworkSettings.cidrHelp: Enter CIDR ranges (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8). When empty, standard private IP ranges are used.

mcpPermissionManagement.panelSubtitle: Configure access permissions and security settings (Optional)
mcpPermissionManagement.allowAllKeysTooltip: When enabled, every API key can access this MCP server.
mcpPermissionManagement.allowAllKeysDesc: Enable if this server should be "public" to all keys.
mcpPermissionManagement.internalNetworkOnlyTooltip: When on, only requests from within your internal network are accepted. Turn off to allow external clients (other clusters, ChatGPT, etc). API key authentication is always required regardless of this setting.
mcpPermissionManagement.internalNetworkOnlyDesc: Turn on to restrict access to callers within your internal network only.
mcpPermissionManagement.delegateAuthTooltip: When on, LiteLLM skips its own API key/SSO check for this server and lets the client complete PKCE directly with the upstream MCP server. Only honored when Auth Type is oauth2. No spend tracking or per-key rate limiting will run on this route.
mcpPermissionManagement.delegateAuthDesc: Bypass LiteLLM auth so clients authenticate directly with the upstream OAuth MCP server.
mcpPermissionManagement.oauthPassthroughTooltip: When on, this server is treated as an OAuth pass-through: the gateway proxies the upstream /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource metadata, emits spec-compliant 401 challenges when no bearer is supplied, and propagates upstream 401/403 responses. Only honored when Auth Type is None and 'Authorization' is in Extra Headers.
mcpPermissionManagement.oauthPassthroughDesc: Forward upstream OAuth discovery and 401 challenges so clients negotiate OAuth directly with the upstream MCP server.
mcpPermissionManagement.internalDelegateWarningTitle: Internal server with upstream OAuth delegation
mcpPermissionManagement.internalDelegateWarningDesc: This MCP server is configured as internal-only but delegates auth to upstream. Anonymous users will be able to reach the upstream OAuth2 /authorize flow without a LiteLLM session. Ensure your upstream provider and network enforce access controls.
mcpPermissionManagement.accessGroupsTooltip: Specify access groups for this MCP server. Users must be in at least one of these groups to access the server.
mcpPermissionManagement.accessGroupsPlaceholder: Select existing groups or type to create new ones
mcpPermissionManagement.extraHeadersTooltip: Forward custom headers from incoming requests to this MCP server (e.g., Authorization, X-Custom-Header, User-Agent)
mcpPermissionManagement.staticHeadersTooltip: Send these key-value headers with every request to this MCP server.

mcpStandardsSettings.fieldSourceUrlDesc: Must link to a source repository

mCPSubmissionsTab.rejectBodyActive: This server is currently live. Rejecting it will immediately remove it from the proxy runtime.
mCPSubmissionsTab.rejectBodyInactive: This will mark the submission as rejected.
mCPSubmissionsTab.confirmAreYouSure: Are you sure you want to {{action}}
mCPSubmissionsTab.confirmApproveBody: This will make it active and available for use.
mCPSubmissionsTab.submissionRulesDesc: Select which fields must be filled in before a submission is considered compliant. LiteLLM will show ✓ / ✗ for each rule on every submission card below.
mCPSubmissionsTab.noSubmissionsMatch: No MCP server submissions match your filters.

mCPToolArgumentsForm.inputRequired: Please enter input for this tool
mCPToolArgumentsForm.noParameters: No parameters required for this tool.

mCPToolsetsTab.noToolsFound: No tools found for this server.
mCPToolsetsTab.pageSubtitle: Curated collections of tools from one or more MCP servers. Assign toolsets to keys and teams via the MCP permissions dropdown.
mCPToolsetsTab.noToolsets: No toolsets yet. Click 'New Toolset' to create one.
mCPToolsetsTab.deleteToolsetConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this toolset? Keys and teams using it will lose access to the scoped tools.
mCPToolsetsTab.howToolsetsWorkDesc1: Create a toolset, assign it to a key via
mCPToolsetsTab.howToolsetsWorkPath: API Keys → Edit Key → MCP Servers
mCPToolsetsTab.howToolsetsWorkDesc2: , then point your MCP client at the toolset URL. The client only sees the tools you picked.

oAuthFormFields.flowTypeTooltip: Choose how the proxy authenticates with this MCP server. M2M is for server-to-server communication using client credentials. Interactive (PKCE) is for user-facing flows that require browser-based authorization.
oAuthFormFields.clientIdM2MTooltip: OAuth2 client ID for the client_credentials grant.
oAuthFormFields.clientIdM2MRequired: Client ID is required for M2M OAuth
oAuthFormFields.clientSecretM2MTooltip: OAuth2 client secret for the client_credentials grant.
oAuthFormFields.clientSecretM2MRequired: Client Secret is required for M2M OAuth
oAuthFormFields.tokenUrlM2MTooltip: Token endpoint URL for the client_credentials grant.
oAuthFormFields.tokenUrlM2MRequired: Token URL is required for M2M OAuth
oAuthFormFields.scopesM2MTooltip: Optional scopes to request with the client_credentials grant.
oAuthFormFields.scopesInteractiveTooltip: Optional scopes requested during token exchange. Separate multiple scopes with enter or commas.
oAuthFormFields.clientIdInteractiveTooltip: Provide only if your MCP server cannot handle dynamic client registration.
oAuthFormFields.clientSecretInteractiveTooltip: Provide only if your MCP server cannot handle dynamic client registration.
oAuthFormFields.authorizationUrlTooltip: Optional override for the authorization endpoint.
oAuthFormFields.tokenUrlInteractiveTooltip: Optional override for the token endpoint.
oAuthFormFields.registrationUrlTooltip: Optional override for the dynamic client registration endpoint.
oAuthFormFields.tokenValidationRulesTooltip: JSON object of key-value rules checked against the OAuth token response before storing. Supports dot-notation for nested fields (e.g. {"organization": "my-org", "team.id": "123"}). Tokens that fail validation are rejected with HTTP 403.
oAuthFormFields.tokenStorageTtlTooltip: How long to cache each user's OAuth access token in Redis before evicting it (regardless of the token's own expires_in). Leave blank to derive the TTL from the token's expires_in, or fall back to the 12-hour default.
oAuthFormFields.oauthFlowDescription: Use OAuth to fetch a fresh access token and temporarily save it in the session as the authentication value.

openAPIFormSection.specUrlTooltip: URL to an OpenAPI specification (JSON or YAML). MCP tools will be automatically generated from the API endpoints defined in the spec.
openAPIFormSection.specUrlRequired: Please enter an OpenAPI spec URL

openAPIQuickPicker.selectHint: Select an API to pre-fill the spec URL and OAuth 2.0 settings, or enter your own spec URL below.

stdioConfiguration.label: Stdio Configuration (JSON)
stdioConfiguration.tooltip: Paste your stdio MCP server configuration in JSON format. You can use the full mcpServers structure from config.yaml or just the inner server configuration.

userEnvVarsModal.title: Set your credentials
userEnvVarsModal.noFieldsConfigured: No per-user fields configured for this server.
userEnvVarsModal.privacyNote: These values are private to you. Your admin configured this MCP server to require these per-user credentials. Saved values are never shown back; leave an already-set field blank to keep it, or enter a value to set or change it.
userEnvVarsModal.overwritePlaceholder: Enter a new value to overwrite

createMcpServer.oauthAuthSuccess: OAuth authorization successful! Please click 'Create MCP Server' to save the configuration.
createMcpServer.invalidJsonTokenValidation: Invalid JSON in Token Validation Rules
createMcpServer.submitSuccess: MCP Server submitted for admin review
createMcpServer.nonAdminBanner: Your submission will be sent for admin review before it becomes active. Note: the request must be made with a team-scoped API key.
createMcpServer.serverNameTooltip: Best practice: Use a descriptive name that indicates the server's purpose (e.g., 'GitHub_MCP', 'Email_Service'). Cannot contain spaces or hyphens; use underscores instead. Names must comply with SEP-986 and will be rejected if invalid (https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/server/tools#tool-names).
createMcpServer.aliasTooltip: A short, unique identifier for this server. Defaults to the server name if not provided. Cannot contain spaces or hyphens; use underscores instead.
createMcpServer.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of what this server does
createMcpServer.byokTooltip: When enabled, each user provides their own API key for this service. Keys are stored per-user and never shared.
createMcpServer.byokKeysSentAs: User keys will be sent as:
createMcpServer.byokSetAuthType: Set the Authentication Type below to specify how user keys are sent (e.g., Bearer Token, API Key header).
createMcpServer.byokAccessDescTooltip: List of permissions shown to users in the connection modal (e.g. 'Create and manage Jira issues')
createMcpServer.byokAccessDescPlaceholder: Add access description items (press Enter after each)
createMcpServer.byokApiKeyHelpUrlTooltip: Optional link shown to users to help them find their API key
createMcpServer.authValueTooltip: Token, password, or header value to send with each request for the selected auth type.
createMcpServer.authValueEmpty: Authentication value cannot be empty whitespace
createMcpServer.awsSigv4Desc: For MCP servers hosted on AWS Bedrock AgentCore. View docs
createMcpServer.awsRegionTooltip: AWS region for SigV4 signing (e.g., us-east-1)
createMcpServer.awsRegionRequired: AWS region is required for SigV4 auth
createMcpServer.awsServiceNameTooltip: AWS service name for SigV4 signing. Defaults to 'bedrock-agentcore'.
createMcpServer.awsAccessKeyIdTooltip: Optional. If not provided, falls back to the boto3 credential chain (IAM role, env vars, etc.).
createMcpServer.awsAccessKeyIdRequired: Access Key ID is required when Secret Access Key is provided
createMcpServer.awsAccessKeyIdPlaceholder: AKIA... (optional — uses IAM role if blank)
createMcpServer.awsSecretKeyTooltip: Optional. Required if AWS Access Key ID is provided.
createMcpServer.awsSecretKeyRequired: Secret Access Key is required when Access Key ID is provided
createMcpServer.awsSecretKeyPlaceholder: Enter secret key (optional — uses IAM role if blank)
createMcpServer.awsSessionTokenTooltip: Optional. Only needed for temporary STS credentials.
createMcpServer.awsRoleArnTooltip: Optional. IAM role ARN to assume via STS before signing. If set, LiteLLM calls sts:AssumeRole to get temporary credentials. Uses ambient credentials (IAM role, env vars) as the source identity unless explicit keys are also provided.
createMcpServer.awsSessionNameTooltip: Optional. Session name for the AssumeRole call — appears in CloudTrail logs. Auto-generated if omitted.
